facilities.title: Fu Ka Care Center
facilities.editorial:
Fu Ka Care Center, operating under Fu Ka Nursing Care Center Sdn. Bhd. (registration 1512468W), is a private nursing home established in May 2023 and located at PTD 14521, Jalan Persiaran Kempas Baru, Kempas Baru, Johor Bahru. The Kempas Baru corridor is a growing residential district in south-central JB, well served by Kempas Medical Centre nearby, and offers relatively newer infrastructure compared to the more congested Johor Jaya and Tampoi nursing home clusters. As a newer entrant, the facility had not yet appeared in major Malaysian elder-care directories at the time of writing.

Being founded in 2023 makes Fu Ka one of the newer private nursing homes in JB, which has implications in both directions: the physical premises, furnishings and care protocols are likely current, but the facility has a shorter track record to evaluate and fewer public reviews to draw from. The 5.0-star Google rating from 24 reviews is encouraging, and the consistency across a post-launch review period suggests early residents and visiting families have had good experiences. Pricing is not published and is quoted on direct enquiry.

When you contact Fu Ka, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and issue date — newer facilities may be at different stages in the licensing process. Ask about the current number of residents, total bed capacity, and the care types accepted: nursing care, assisted living, dementia, palliative. Confirm the nurse-to-resident ratio for day and night shifts, and ask whether a doctor makes scheduled ward rounds or is available on-call only. The Kempas Baru location provides access to Kempas Medical Centre and is approximately 20 to 25 minutes from Hospital Sultan Ismail and KPJ Johor Specialist Hospital.

facilities.title: Sunstar Golden Care Centre
facilities.editorial:
Sunstar Golden Care Centre is a nursing home in Bandar Sunway — one of Selangor's most densely served eldercare corridors, given its proximity to Sunway Medical Centre and the large residential population across Bandar Sunway, Subang Jaya and Puchong. The facility holds a 5.0-star Google rating from 24 reviews, a consistent score that signals a pattern of satisfied residents and visiting family members. Beyond the Google Maps listing, the home does not maintain a published website or active social media presence, so most service details must be gathered through direct contact.

The absence of a public website is not itself a negative indicator — many small, well-run private nursing homes in Malaysia operate entirely by word of mouth and family referral. It does mean that families need to do more information-gathering on the phone and in person before making a placement decision. Pricing is not published and is quoted on enquiry. Specific details such as founding year, operator name, bed capacity and JKM licence number are not available from public listings.

When you contact Sunstar Golden Care Centre, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and its current validity status. Ask about the total number of beds, the number of residents currently in the home, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for both day and night shifts. Clarify which care conditions are accepted — assisted living, nursing care, dementia, palliative, post-surgery recovery — and whether physiotherapy is provided. Ask how the monthly fee is structured and whether incontinence supplies, medication and specialist visits are included or billed separately. A site visit is strongly recommended before making a placement decision.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Sri Sentosa
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Sri Sentosa is a small private care home with a listed capacity of 15 residents, situated in Taman Sentosa, Johor Bahru. With 15 beds, this is a compact, community-home-scale facility where the resident-to-staff dynamic is typically more personal than in a large institutional nursing home. Taman Sentosa is a well-established residential neighbourhood within JB, accessible from most parts of the city. Founding year, operator name and JKM licence details are not stated in public listings.

The 3.6-star Google rating from 23 reviews sits below the typical market average for JB-area nursing homes, which tend to cluster between 4.0 and 4.7. The sample of 23 reviews is meaningful enough to reflect an accumulated pattern of experiences rather than a single incident. A rating at this level can arise from a range of underlying factors — staffing gaps, communication issues with visiting family members, physical facility constraints, or a difficult transition period — and the way to understand the specifics is through a direct visit and conversation with both staff and current residents' families. Families considering this home are advised to use that information as a prompt for extra due diligence before committing.

When you visit or contact Pusat Jagaan Sri Sentosa, ask about the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence status, the nurse-to-resident ratio for day and overnight shifts, and how clinical escalation is handled when a resident's condition deteriorates. Ask to speak with the care manager and, if possible, with a family member of a current resident. Confirm the pricing model, the care conditions accepted, and whether the home has experienced significant staff turnover in the past year. Given the 15-bed scale, understanding who specifically is on duty overnight is a particularly important question.

facilities.title: Prestige Residence Care (Group) Sdn Bhd
facilities.editorial:
Prestige Residence Care (Group) Sdn Bhd is a private nursing operator in Johor Bahru serving elderly and physically disabled residents. The operator's website lists core services — 24-hour nursing care, an on-call doctor arrangement, ambulance coordination, and physiotherapy — and describes room configurations of 2-bed and 4-bed setups, suggesting a small to mid-sized facility. Visiting hours are stated as daily from 11am to 6pm, a standard window for private JB nursing homes. The facility address, total bed count and JKM licence number are not published on the website.

The 4.4-star Google rating from 22 reviews is solid for a JB-area private nursing home of this profile. Pricing is not published and is quoted on direct enquiry. The operator name includes "Group" in the branding, though whether this reflects multiple sites or is aspirational branding for a single facility is not stated publicly — a useful clarification to seek on your initial call. The care scope described — 24-hour nursing, on-call doctor, physiotherapy and ambulance arrangement — covers the core clinical needs of most nursing home residents if it is staffed as advertised.

When you contact Prestige Residence Care, ask for the full facility address, the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and expiry, the total number of beds and current occupancy, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. Clarify whether physiotherapy is provided by a qualified in-house physiotherapist or via an external on-call contractor. Ask how the on-call doctor arrangement works in practice — response time, hospital affiliation, and whether a doctor makes scheduled ward rounds. Confirm the pricing structure and ask whether supplementary fees apply beyond the headline monthly rate.

facilities.title: Blissful Senior Care Centre
facilities.editorial:
Blissful Senior Care Centre is one of Kluang's licensed residential care options for older adults. Kluang is a town of approximately 323,000 residents in central Johor, roughly 100 km north of Johor Bahru and positioned as a more affordable alternative to the saturated urban eldercare market along the JB-Skudai-Permas corridor. The facility emphasises its government licensing and certification prominently — visible in the listing name itself — signalling a deliberate positioning against unlicensed care homes that continue to operate across the peninsula.

The 4.8-star Google rating from 22 reviews is strong for a Kluang-area facility, where review volumes are typically lower than in the JB metro due to smaller population and slower adoption of online reviews among the older demographic. For families based in Kluang, Segamat or the interior Johor corridor, a strong regional option removes the need to transport an elderly family member into the congested JB nursing home market. Pricing is not published and is quoted on direct contact. Specific details such as founding year, total bed capacity and JKM licence number are not available from public listings but should be confirmed before placement.

When you contact Blissful Senior Care Centre, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and expiry date, which confirms the licensing claim is current rather than historical. Ask about total bed capacity, current occupancy and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. Confirm which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube feeding, bedbound residents — and whether physiotherapy is provided in-house. Ask whether there is a visiting or on-call doctor arrangement. For families in the interior Johor towns, the Kluang location also avoids the impractical visiting distances that a JB placement would involve.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Rumah Kebajikan Calvary Land
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Rumah Kebajikan Calvary Land is part of Calvaryland, an integrated social welfare campus operated by Calvary Church of Malaysia in Sungai Pelek, Sepang district, Selangor. Set on 10 acres of eco-friendly land approximately 50 km south of Kuala Lumpur — roughly midway between KL and Port Dickson — the campus brings together multiple residential welfare programmes: a home for older adults, a children's home and a women-in-crisis shelter, all under one roof. The facility has been registered with the Selangor Social Welfare Department since 2008.

The multi-programme, faith-based campus model distinguishes Calvary Land from conventional private nursing homes. Care here operates within a Christian organisational framework, and elderly residents are part of a broader welfare community that includes children and individuals from crisis situations — an unusual intergenerational setting. Whether that environment is a positive or a constraint for a given resident depends on the individual's background and preferences, and is worth exploring during a site visit. As a faith-based welfare facility, the cost structure and any subsidy arrangements differ from commercial nursing homes and are worth clarifying in your initial contact. The 4.4-star Google rating from 22 reviews is consistent.

The Sungai Pelek location is peaceful and spacious, but it is a significant distance from major hospitals in KL or Seremban — a consideration for residents with active medical conditions requiring regular specialist follow-up. Families based in Sepang, Nilai or the coastal Selangor corridor will find the home more accessible than families in urban KL. On your call, ask about the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence for the elderly arm, current resident capacity and the intake process, nursing staff qualifications and overnight coverage, and how medical emergencies are handled given the rural location. Also confirm whether placement is open to non-Christians.

facilities.title: Sahana Old Folks Home
facilities.editorial:
Sahana Old Folks Home is at Jalan Selampit 23, Taman Klang Jaya, Klang — a residential neighbourhood in the heart of Klang town, approximately 2.5 km from Bukit Tinggi Medical Centre (Manipal Hospitals Klang) and within similar reach of Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah, Klang's principal government hospital. That dual-hospital proximity is a genuine clinical advantage for nursing home residents with active medical conditions who require regular specialist input or emergency transfer capability.

The facility is listed under the care categories of nursing home, physical therapy and retirement home — suggesting a service scope that spans from lower-dependency retirement living through to full nursing care with physiotherapy. The 5.0-star Google rating from 21 reviews is clean and consistent. Pricing is not published and is quoted on direct enquiry. Founding year, bed capacity, JKM licence details and staffing information are not stated in public listings and should all be confirmed before placement. The Klang Jaya location also gives reasonable access for families based across the Klang-Meru-Kapar corridor.

When you contact Sahana Old Folks Home, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and its current status, the total bed count and current occupancy, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for day and overnight shifts. Clarify whether physiotherapy is provided by a qualified in-house physiotherapist or is arranged through an external contractor. Ask which care conditions are accepted beyond assisted living — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, bedbound residents — and confirm the monthly pricing structure, including what the base fee covers and whether medication, incontinence supplies and specialist visits are itemised separately. A site visit before committing to placement is recommended.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Lekshmy Illam Nursing Home
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Lekshmy Illam Nursing Home is at No. 89, Jalan Bersatu 4, Taman Bersatu, Rawang — a township roughly 25 km north of Kuala Lumpur along the Rawang-Serendah corridor. The name 'Lekshmy Illam' is Tamil; 'illam' means home, and available contact details point to an Indian-managed operation. The home is likely to be particularly relevant for Indian families in the Rawang-Selayang-Kepong corridor seeking a care environment with Tamil-language communication, Indian cultural norms and familiar food — dimensions of care comfort that matter considerably for elderly residents, especially those with limited Mandarin or English fluency.

The 4.6-star Google rating from 21 reviews is solid for a community-scale nursing home in a smaller corridor town like Rawang, where review volumes tend to be lower than in major urban centres. Pricing is not published and is quoted on direct enquiry. The home's admissions policy — whether it is open to all communities or prioritises Indian residents — is not stated publicly and is a relevant point to clarify on your initial call. Founding year, bed capacity, JKM licence number and staffing details are not available from public listings.

For Indian families managing the care of an elderly parent in the Klang Valley north corridor, a home with Tamil-speaking staff and culturally familiar food can meaningfully reduce the stress of a placement. When you contact Lekshmy Illam, ask about language coverage among care staff, the dietary options available, and whether religious observances relevant to Hindu or Christian Indian families can be accommodated. Also ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number, total bed count and current occupancy, the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts, and which care conditions — dementia, bedbound, palliative — are accepted.

facilities.title: 2E Nursing & Healthcare
facilities.editorial:
2E Nursing & Healthcare appears on Google Maps as a nursing home in Kuala Lumpur, also listed under healthcare and medical service categories. The facility holds a 5.0-star Google rating from 20 reviews. Beyond the listing and a contact number, no public website, founding information, operator name or service brochure is available — which means families will need to gather all service details through direct contact before making a placement decision.

A 5.0-star score from 20 reviews indicates consistency rather than a fluke: a single highly-rated review cannot sustain a perfect average across two dozen entries. That said, the limited public presence makes independent verification of clinical capabilities, licensing status and staffing arrangements especially important here. Pricing is quoted on enquiry. The KL address is noted in the listing, but the precise location within KL — which determines practical hospital proximity and visiting convenience — is best confirmed when you make contact.

When you contact 2E Nursing & Healthcare, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and expiry date, the facility address and immediate neighbourhood, total bed capacity and current occupancy, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for day and night shifts. Clarify which care conditions are accepted — assisted living, nursing care, dementia, palliative, PEG tube — and ask how medical escalation is handled. Confirm the monthly pricing structure and what is included in the base fee. Given the limited public footprint, asking to arrange a visit before committing is essential — a reputable home will welcome the request without hesitation.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan En Yuan PLT (Kg Cheras Baru)
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan En Yuan PLT operates from 5/5A Jalan 28, Kampung Cheras Baru, 56100 Kuala Lumpur — a part of the Cheras Baru terrace-house belt that hosts a cluster of small Chinese-run nursing homes. The operator is registered as a Perkongsian Liabiliti Terhad (limited-liability partnership) under SSM. The Mandarin name — En Yuan, broadly meaning grace and affinity — signals a Chinese-community operating character, and the terrace-house setting is typical for this pocket of Cheras Baru, where converted linked houses serve as the premises for several neighbouring care facilities.

Listings classify En Yuan variously as an assisted-living facility and a nursing home, suggesting a mixed dependency range rather than a single-tier service. The 4.2-star Google rating from 20 reviews is a competent mid-range result — not exceptional, but without an obvious pattern of concern. Pricing is not published and is quoted on direct enquiry. The Kampung Cheras Baru location gives reasonable access from the Cheras and Pandan corridors of KL, and the cluster of nearby nursing homes means families can compare options within a short walking or driving radius before deciding.

When you contact Pusat Jagaan En Yuan, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and status, the total bed count and current occupancy across both floors if the property is a double-storey terrace, and whether lift or hoist access is available for residents with limited mobility. Ask about the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts, which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, bedbound residents — and how clinical escalation is managed. Confirm the monthly pricing model and what the base fee covers. A visit to see the physical layout, including bedroom size and bathroom accessibility, is strongly recommended before placement.
